mnemonics.title: Random Pivot Selection
mnemonics.content: RAPE: Randomly Arrange Pivot Every time - Remember to randomly select pivot to avoid worst-case partitioning

mnemonics.title: Quicksort Parts
mnemonics.content: PARC: Partition - Arrange elements around pivot - Recurse on subarrays - Combine (implicitly, as it's in-place)

acronyms.expansion: Last Element Mostly Used Together - Partition scheme using last element as pivot
acronyms.usage: Naming the partition method where last element serves as pivot

acronyms.expansion: High Order Algorithm for Quick Recursive Exchange - Original quicksort partition
acronyms.usage: Naming the two-pointer partition scheme developed by Tony Hoare

acronyms.expansion: Dutch National Flag partition - divides into Less, Equal, Greater
acronyms.usage: Handling duplicate elements efficiently in quicksort

visualTips.visualization: Imagine picking a random card from a shuffled deck to split the deck into two piles - you don't know if piles will be equal but statistically they tend to be balanced
Think of it like arranging students by height around a chosen pivot student - smaller go left, taller go right
Draw an inverted tree where root is full array, branches split randomly; average tree has ~log n height, worst-case has n height like a vine

analogies.analogy: Like choosing a random person in a sorted line to split the line - you won't always get perfectly balanced halves but statistically it works well
Like organizing books on a shelf by sliding them left or right rather than taking them all out and using another shelf
Like shuffling a deck before cutting - if you always cut at the same position, an adversary can stack the deck to give you bad cuts
